title: It started with a birthday present — why I built an open-source QR code generator
excerpt: Last year, all I wanted was a QR code for a birthday present. Scan the code, get the surprise. Thirty...
content_html:
Last year, all I wanted was a QR code for a birthday present. Scan the code, get the surprise. Thirty seconds of work, I thought.

Not quite.

Every single generator wanted me to create an account first — for what is, technically, a black-and-white square with some data encoded inside. The ones that skipped the sign-up put a watermark on the download or hid the decent export quality behind a paywall. And the moment I looked at dynamic QR codes — the kind where you can change the target URL even after the code is printed — every site wanted a subscription.

I got the birthday present done in the end. But the experience stuck with me, and I did the thing you should probably never do with a minor annoyance: I turned it into a side project.

The "free QR code" business model

Once you look closer, the strategy behind most QR generators is remarkably consistent:

  1. You google "free QR code generator" and click one of the top results.
  2. You generate a code — often a dynamic one by default, whether you notice or not. It doesn't point to your website; it points to their redirect service.
  3. You download it and print it on flyers, menus, packaging, a gift.
  4. The trial ends. The redirect now leads to an upgrade page.
  5. Your printed material is now a billboard for someone else's pricing page. Your options: pay, or reprint everything.

None of this is illegal — it's all in the fine print. Which somehow makes it worse. It works precisely because most people don't know the difference between a static and a dynamic QR code. The switching cost isn't technical, it's physical: ink on paper.

A dynamic QR code is a 302 redirect

Here's the part that bothered me as a developer.

A static QR code is just data encoded into pixels. The URL (or Wi-Fi credentials, or contact card) lives inside the image. It works forever, nobody can turn it off, and it costs practically nothing to generate.

A dynamic QR code is a static QR code that points to a URL shortener:

Static:   [QR] ──encodes──▶ https://your-site.com
Dynamic:  [QR] ──encodes──▶ https://short.link/u/abc123 ──302──▶ wherever you want (editable)

That's the whole product. The premium feature that costs $15 a month in many places is a database row and an HTTP redirect.

To be fair: running servers, analytics dashboards and support costs real money. But the gap between what this costs to operate and what's being charged — combined with the expiry tactics — was big enough that I wanted an alternative to exist.

QRcodly

QRcodly is an open-source (MIT) QR code generator and management platform. The free tier is actually free:

  • Unlimited static and dynamic QR codes, unlimited scans
  • Custom styling, colors and logo uploads
  • Scan analytics
  • A built-in URL shortener
  • No credit card, no expiry tricks

How does it stay sustainable? There's a Pro tier with business features: your own domain on short links, REST API access, bulk imports, third-party integrations. Companies pay for company features. Individuals don't pay ransom for a redirect.

And because it's MIT-licensed, the "no tricks" promise isn't a marketing slogan — it's enforceable. If I ever pull an expiry stunt, you can fork the repo and self-host it. The license is the guarantee.

Why not just Next.js?

Fair question. A QR code generator is mostly forms, and Next.js alone would have carried that just fine.

Two things made me set it up differently. First, I knew early on this wouldn't stay a website. I wanted a browser extension, and at some point a desktop app — so I committed to TypeScript everywhere: one language, and more importantly one set of types, across every surface.

Second, some of the work simply isn't request/response-shaped. Rendering styled QR codes server-side (jsdom + resvg + sharp), running scheduled jobs, reacting to things like "user deleted" or "subscription past due" — for that I wanted a long-running server I fully control, not API routes that time out mid-render. So the backend is a separate Fastify REST API. And honestly, there was a third reason: I'd heard a lot about Fastify's performance and wanted an excuse to finally try it. (Verdict: the hype is justified.)

One thing I deliberately avoided: Kafka, RabbitMQ, or any external message broker. At this scale that's operational overhead with no payoff — and every extra piece of infrastructure makes self-hosting harder, which actually matters for an open-source project. Instead I built a small event system straight into the backend: a decorator-based emitter where a class tagged with @EventHandler(UserDeletedEvent.eventName) simply handles that event, plus cron jobs guarded by a Redis lock so they don't double-run across instances. Boring, self-contained, zero extra moving parts.

All of it lives in one pnpm monorepo: frontend, backend and browser extension as apps, plus shared packages they all import. When the desktop app lands, it plugs into the exact same setup.

The best decision: one shared schema package

The glue of that monorepo is a shared package holding Zod schemas as the single source of truth for every QR code content type:

export const QrCodeContent = z.discriminatedUnion('type', [
    createContentSchema('url'),
    createContentSchema('text'),
    createContentSchema('wifi'),
    createContentSchema('vCard'),
    createContentSchema('email'),
    createContentSchema('location'),
    createContentSchema('event'),
    createContentSchema('epc'), // SEPA payment QR codes
]);

The same schemas validate the React forms in the frontend and the request bodies in Fastify, and they generate all the TypeScript types on both sides. Adding a new content type means: write a schema, write a converter, build the form. Everything else falls out of the types. With the web app, the API and the browser extension all consuming this package — and the desktop app next — that saves an absurd amount of duplicated code.

The rest of the stack: Drizzle ORM on MySQL, Redis for caching, Clerk for auth, S3-compatible storage for logo uploads, and next-intl for nine languages. Dynamic QR codes are — true to the analysis above — a URL shortener: middleware catches /u/:shortCode, the backend records the scan and 302s you to the target.

I'm planning to write more about the individual pieces (building the URL shortener, the shared-schema setup, self-hosting the whole thing). Tell me in the comments which one you'd actually read.

One year later

A year in, QRcodly is stable and the first paying customers are in — and I've learned that building the product was maybe half the job. The other half is making sure anyone finds it. This post is, in part, me working on that second half.

If this resonated: I built QRcodly, an open-source QR code platform with free static and dynamic QR codes. Try it, or come contribute:

  • 🔗 qrcodly.de
  • ⭐ github.com/FloB95/qrcodly

QRcodly

QRcodly is a free, open-source QR code generator and management platform. Create, customize, and track QR codes for URLs, contact details, Wi-Fi credentials, and more.

Features

  • Multiple QR code types — URL, vCard, Wi-Fi, Email, Calendar Event, Location, Plain Text
  • Full customization — colors, sizes, backgrounds, and custom icon uploads
  • Export formats — PNG, JPEG, SVG
  • URL shortening & analytics — shorten links and track scans
  • Templates — save and reuse QR code configurations
  • Custom domains — use your own domain for short URLs (Cloudflare integration)
  • Internationalization — 8 languages (EN, DE, ES, FR, IT, NL, PL, RU)
  • Authentication — powered by Clerk
  • Browser extension — generate QR codes from any page

Monorepo Structure

qrcodly/
├── apps/
│   ├── backend/            # Fastify REST API
│   ├── frontend/           # Next.js web application
│   └── browser-extension/  # Vite-based browser extension
├── packages/
│   ├── shared/             # Zod schemas, DTOs, and shared

…

And I'm curious: what's the worst "free until you depend on it" trick a tool has ever pulled on you?

So the backend is a separate Fastify REST API. And honestly, there was a third reason: I'd heard a lot about Fastify's performance and wanted an excuse to finally try it. (Verdict: the hype is justified.)

sentences.en: Second, some of the work simply isn't request/response-shaped.
sentences.zh: 其次，有些工作根本不是请求/响应形式的。

sentences.en: Rendering styled QR codes server-side (jsdom + resvg + sharp), running scheduled jobs, reacting to things like "user deleted" or "subscription past due" — for that I wanted a long-running server I fully control, not API routes that time out mid-render.
sentences.zh: 在服务器端渲染风格的 QR 码（jsdom + resvg + Sharp），运行计划作业，对“用户删除”或“过期订阅”等内容做出反应 - 为此，我想要一个完全控制的长期运行的服务器，而不是在渲染中超时的 API 路由。

sentences.en: So the backend is a separate Fastify REST API.
sentences.zh: 所以后端是一个单独的 Fastify REST API。

sentences.en: And honestly, there was a third reason: I'd heard a lot about Fastify's performance and wanted an excuse to finally try it.
sentences.zh: 老实说，还有第三个原因：我听说过很多有关 Fastify 性能的信息，并且想要一个最终尝试它的借口。

sentences.en: (Verdict: the hype is justified.)
sentences.zh: （结论：炒作是有道理的。）

blocks.html_en: One thing I deliberately avoided: Kafka, RabbitMQ, or any external message broker. At this scale that's operational overhead with no payoff — and every extra piece of infrastructure makes self-hosting harder, which actually matters for an open-source project. Instead I built a small event system straight into the backend: a decorator-based emitter where a class tagged with @EventHandler(UserDeletedEvent.eventName) simply handles that event, plus cron jobs guarded by a Redis lock so they don't double-run across instances. Boring, self-contained, zero extra moving parts.

sentences.en: One thing I deliberately avoided: Kafka, RabbitMQ, or any external message broker.
sentences.zh: 我故意避免的一件事：Kafka、RabbitMQ 或任何外部消息代理。

sentences.en: At this scale that's operational overhead with no payoff — and every extra piece of infrastructure makes self-hosting harder, which actually matters for an open-source project.
sentences.zh: 在这种规模下，运营开销是没有回报的——而且每一个额外的基础设施都会使自托管变得更加困难，这对于开源项目来说实际上很重要。

sentences.en: Instead I built a small event system straight into the backend: a decorator-based emitter where a class tagged with @EventHandler(UserDeletedEvent.eventName) simply handles that event, plus cron jobs guarded by a Redis lock so they don't double-run across instances.
sentences.zh: 相反，我直接在后端构建了一个小型事件系统：一个基于装饰器的发射器，其中标有 @EventHandler(UserDeletedEvent.eventName) 的类仅处理该事件，加上由 Redis 锁保护的 cron 作业，因此它们不会跨实例双重运行。

sentences.en: Boring, self-contained, zero extra moving parts.
sentences.zh: 无聊、独立、零额外的移动部件。

blocks.html_en: All of it lives in one pnpm monorepo: frontend, backend and browser extension as apps, plus shared packages they all import. When the desktop app lands, it plugs into the exact same setup.

sentences.en: All of it lives in one pnpm monorepo: frontend, backend and browser extension as apps, plus shared packages they all import.
sentences.zh: 所有这些都位于一个 pnpm monorepo 中：作为应用程序的前端、后端和浏览器扩展，以及它们全部导入的共享包。

sentences.en: When the desktop app lands, it plugs into the exact same setup.
sentences.zh: 当桌面应用程序登陆时，它会插入完全相同的设置。

blocks.html_en: The best decision: one shared schema package

sentences.en: The best decision: one shared schema package
sentences.zh: 最佳决策：一个共享模式包

blocks.html_en: The glue of that monorepo is a shared package holding Zod schemas as the single source of truth for every QR code content type:

sentences.en: The glue of that monorepo is a shared package holding Zod schemas as the single source of truth for every QR code content type:
sentences.zh: 该 monorepo 的粘合剂是一个共享包，其中包含 Zod 模式作为每个 QR 码内容类型的单一事实来源：

sentences.en: Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode
sentences.zh: 进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

blocks.html_en: The same schemas validate the React forms in the frontend and the request bodies in Fastify, and they generate all the TypeScript types on both sides. Adding a new content type means: write a schema, write a converter, build the form. Everything else falls out of the types. With the web app, the API and the browser extension all consuming this package — and the desktop app next — that saves an absurd amount of duplicated code.

sentences.en: The same schemas validate the React forms in the frontend and the request bodies in Fastify, and they generate all the TypeScript types on both sides.
sentences.zh: 相同的模式验证前端中的 React 表单和 Fastify 中的请求主体，并且它们在两侧生成所有 TypeScript 类型。

sentences.en: Adding a new content type means: write a schema, write a converter, build the form.
sentences.zh: 添加新的内容类型意味着：编写架构、编写转换器、构建表单。

sentences.en: Everything else falls out of the types.
sentences.zh: 其他一切都不属于类型。

sentences.en: With the web app, the API and the browser extension all consuming this package — and the desktop app next — that saves an absurd amount of duplicated code.
sentences.zh: Web 应用程序、API 和浏览器扩展都消耗这个包——接下来是桌面应用程序——这可以节省大量的重复代码。

blocks.html_en: The rest of the stack: Drizzle ORM on MySQL, Redis for caching, Clerk for auth, S3-compatible storage for logo uploads, and next-intl for nine languages. Dynamic QR codes are — true to the analysis above — a URL shortener: middleware catches /u/:shortCode, the backend records the scan and 302s you to the target.

sentences.en: The rest of the stack: Drizzle ORM on MySQL, Redis for caching, Clerk for auth, S3-compatible storage for logo uploads, and next-intl for nine languages.
sentences.zh: 堆栈的其余部分：MySQL 上的 Drizzle ORM、用于缓存的 Redis、用于身份验证的 Clerk、用于徽标上传的 S3 兼容存储以及用于九种语言的 next-intl。

sentences.en: Dynamic QR codes are — true to the analysis above — a URL shortener: middleware catches /u/:shortCode, the backend records the scan and 302s you to the target.
sentences.zh: 动态 QR 码（与上面的分析相符）是 URL 缩短器：中间件捕获 /u/:shortCode，后端记录扫描并将您 302 发送到目标。

blocks.html_en: I'm planning to write more about the individual pieces (building the URL shortener, the shared-schema setup, self-hosting the whole thing). Tell me in the comments which one you'd actually read.

sentences.en: I'm planning to write more about the individual pieces (building the URL shortener, the shared-schema setup, self-hosting the whole thing).
sentences.zh: 我计划写更多关于各个部分的内容（构建 URL 缩短器、共享架构设置、自托管整个内容）。

sentences.en: Tell me in the comments which one you'd actually read.
sentences.zh: 在评论中告诉我你真正读过哪一篇。

blocks.html_en: One year later

sentences.en: One year later
sentences.zh: 一年后

blocks.html_en: A year in, QRcodly is stable and the first paying customers are in — and I've learned that building the product was maybe half the job. The other half is making sure anyone finds it. This post is, in part, me working on that second half.

sentences.en: A year in, QRcodly is stable and the first paying customers are in — and I've learned that building the product was maybe half the job.
sentences.zh: 一年后，QRcodly 变得稳定，第一批付费客户也出现了——而且我了解到，构建产品可能已经完成了一半的工作。

sentences.en: The other half is making sure anyone finds it.
sentences.zh: 另一半是确保有人找到它。

sentences.en: This post is, in part, me working on that second half.
sentences.zh: 这篇文章部分是我在后半部分的工作。

blocks.html_en: If this resonated: I built QRcodly, an open-source QR code platform with free static and dynamic QR codes. Try it, or come contribute:

sentences.en: If this resonated: I built QRcodly, an open-source QR code platform with free static and dynamic QR codes.
sentences.zh: 如果这引起了共鸣：我构建了 QRcodly，一个开源 QR 码平台，提供免费的静态和动态 QR 码。

sentences.en: Try it, or come contribute:
sentences.zh: 尝试一下，或者来贡献一下：

blocks.html_en: • 🔗 qrcodly.de

sentences.en: 🔗 qrcodly.de

blocks.html_en: • ⭐ github.com/FloB95/qrcodly

sentences.en: ⭐ github.com/FloB95/qrcodly

blocks.html_en: QRcodly

sentences.en: QRcodly
sentences.zh: 二维码

blocks.html_en: QRcodly is a free, open-source QR code generator and management platform. Create, customize, and track QR codes for URLs, contact details, Wi-Fi credentials, and more.

sentences.en: QRcodly is a free, open-source QR code generator and management platform.
sentences.zh: QRcodly 是一个免费、开源的二维码生成器和管理平台。

sentences.en: Create, customize, and track QR codes for URLs, contact details, Wi-Fi credentials, and more.
sentences.zh: 创建、自定义和跟踪 URL、联系方式、Wi-Fi 凭据等的 QR 码。

blocks.html_en: Features

sentences.en: Features
sentences.zh: 特征

blocks.html_en: • Multiple QR code types — URL, vCard, Wi-Fi, Email, Calendar Event, Location, Plain Text

sentences.en: Multiple QR code types — URL, vCard, Wi-Fi, Email, Calendar Event, Location, Plain Text
sentences.zh: 多种 QR 码类型 — URL、vCard、Wi-Fi、电子邮件、日历事件、位置、纯文本

blocks.html_en: • Full customization — colors, sizes, backgrounds, and custom icon uploads

sentences.en: Full customization — colors, sizes, backgrounds, and custom icon uploads
sentences.zh: 完全定制——颜色、尺寸、背景和定制图标上传

blocks.html_en: • Export formats — PNG, JPEG, SVG

sentences.en: Export formats — PNG, JPEG, SVG
sentences.zh: 导出格式 — PNG、JPEG、SVG

blocks.html_en: • URL shortening & analytics — shorten links and track scans

sentences.en: URL shortening & analytics — shorten links and track scans
sentences.zh: URL 缩短和分析 — 缩短链接并跟踪扫描

blocks.html_en: • Templates — save and reuse QR code configurations

sentences.en: Templates — save and reuse QR code configurations
sentences.zh: 模板——保存和重复使用二维码配置

blocks.html_en: • Custom domains — use your own domain for short URLs (Cloudflare integration)

sentences.en: Custom domains — use your own domain for short URLs (Cloudflare integration)

blocks.html_en: • Internationalization — 8 languages (EN, DE, ES, FR, IT, NL, PL, RU)

sentences.en: Internationalization — 8 languages (EN, DE, ES, FR, IT, NL, PL, RU)

blocks.html_en: • Authentication — powered by Clerk

sentences.en: Authentication — powered by Clerk
sentences.zh: 身份验证 — 由 Clerk 提供支持

sentences.en: Browser extension — generate QR codes from any page

blocks.html_en: Monorepo Structure

sentences.en: Monorepo Structure
sentences.zh: 单一回购结构

blocks.html_en: And I'm curious: what's the worst "free until you depend on it" trick a tool has ever pulled on you?

sentences.en: And I'm curious: what's the worst "free until you depend on it" trick a tool has ever pulled on you?
sentences.zh: 我很好奇：工具给你带来的最糟糕的“免费直到你依赖它”的伎俩是什么？

content_html_zh:
去年，我想要的只是一个二维码作为生日礼物。 扫码，有惊喜。 我想，三十秒的工作。

不完全是。

每个生成器都希望我首先创建一个帐户 - 从技术上讲，该帐户是一个黑白方块，里面编码了一些数据。 那些跳过注册的人在下载时添加了水印，或者将良好的出口质量隐藏在付费专区后面。 当我看到动态二维码（即使在打印代码后也可以更改目标 URL）时，每个网站都想要订阅。

我终于完成了生日礼物。 但这段经历一直困扰着我，我做了一件你可能永远不应该做的事情，因为有点烦恼：我把它变成了一个副项目。

“免费二维码”商业模式

一旦你仔细观察，大多数 QR 生成器背后的策略是非常一致的：

  1. 您在谷歌上搜索“免费二维码生成器”，然后点击最上面的结果之一。
  2. 您生成一个代码——无论您是否注意到，默认情况下通常是动态代码。 它并不指向您的网站；它指向他们的重定向服务。
  3. 您下载它并将其打印在传单、菜单、包装、礼物上。
  4. 审判结束。 重定向现在会引导至升级页面。
  5. 您的印刷材料现在是其他人定价页面的广告牌。 您的选择：付费或重新打印所有内容。

这些都不是非法的——这一切都在细则中。 这在某种程度上让情况变得更糟。 它之所以有效，正是因为大多数人不知道静态二维码和动态二维码之间的区别。 转换成本不是技术性的，而是物理性的：纸上的墨水。

动态 QR 码是 302 重定向

这是让我作为开发人员感到困扰的部分。

静态 QR 码只是编码为像素的数据。 URL（或 Wi-Fi 凭证或名片）位于图像内。 它永远有效，没有人可以将其关闭，并且生成几乎不需要任何成本。

动态 QR 码是指向 URL 缩短器的静态 QR 码：

Static:   [QR] ──encodes──▶ https://your-site.com
Dynamic:  [QR] ──encodes──▶ https://short.link/u/abc123 ──302──▶ wherever you want (editable)

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

这就是整个产品。 在许多地方，每月花费 15 美元的高级功能是数据库行和 HTTP 重定向。

公平地说：运行服务器、分析仪表板和支持需要真金白银。 但运营成本和收费之间的差距（加上到期策略）足够大，以至于我希望存在替代方案。

二维码

QRcodly 是一个开源 (MIT) 的 QR 码生成器和管理平台。 免费套餐实际上是免费的：

  • 无限静态、动态二维码，无限扫描
  • 自定义样式、颜色和徽标上传
  • 扫描分析
  • 内置 URL 缩短器
  • 没有信用卡，没有过期技巧

它如何保持可持续发展？ Pro 层具有业务功能：您自己的短链接域、REST API 访问、批量导入、第三方集成。 公司为公司功能付费。 个人无需为重定向支付赎金。

而且因为它是麻省理工学院许可的，所以“不耍花招”的承诺不是营销口号——而是可执行的。 如果我搞了一个过期特技，你可以分叉存储库并自行托管它。 许可证是保证。

为什么不只是 Next.js？

公平的问题。 QR 码生成器主要是表单，仅 Next.js 就可以很好地实现这一点。

有两件事让我做出了不同的设置。 首先，我很早就知道这不会只是一个网站。 我想要一个浏览器扩展，有时还想要一个桌面应用程序——所以我致力于在任何地方使用 TypeScript：一种语言，更重要的是一组类型，遍布每个表面。

其次，有些工作根本不是请求/响应形式的。 在服务器端渲染风格的 QR 码（jsdom + resvg + Sharp），运行计划作业，对“用户删除”或“过期订阅”等内容做出反应 - 为此，我想要一个完全控制的长期运行的服务器，而不是在渲染中超时的 API 路由。 所以后端是一个单独的 Fastify REST API。 老实说，还有第三个原因：我听说过很多有关 Fastify 性能的信息，并且想要一个最终尝试它的借口。 （结论：炒作是有道理的。）

我故意避免的一件事：Kafka、RabbitMQ 或任何外部消息代理。 在这种规模下，运营开销是没有回报的——而且每一个额外的基础设施都会使自托管变得更加困难，这对于开源项目来说实际上很重要。 相反，我直接在后端构建了一个小型事件系统：一个基于装饰器的发射器，其中标有 @EventHandler(UserDeletedEvent.eventName) 的类仅处理该事件，加上由 Redis 锁保护的 cron 作业，因此它们不会跨实例双重运行。 无聊、独立、零额外的移动部件。

所有这些都位于一个 pnpm monorepo 中：作为应用程序的前端、后端和浏览器扩展，以及它们全部导入的共享包。 当桌面应用程序登陆时，它会插入完全相同的设置。

最佳决策：一个共享模式包

该 monorepo 的粘合剂是一个共享包，其中包含 Zod 模式作为每个 QR 码内容类型的单一事实来源：

export const QrCodeContent = z.discriminatedUnion('type', [
    createContentSchema('url'),
    createContentSchema('text'),
    createContentSchema('wifi'),
    createContentSchema('vCard'),
    createContentSchema('email'),
    createContentSchema('location'),
    createContentSchema('event'),
    createContentSchema('epc'), // SEPA payment QR codes
]);

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

相同的模式验证前端中的 React 表单和 Fastify 中的请求主体，并且它们在两侧生成所有 TypeScript 类型。 添加新的内容类型意味着：编写架构、编写转换器、构建表单。 其他一切都不属于类型。 Web 应用程序、API 和浏览器扩展都消耗这个包——接下来是桌面应用程序——这可以节省大量的重复代码。

堆栈的其余部分：MySQL 上的 Drizzle ORM、用于缓存的 Redis、用于身份验证的 Clerk、用于徽标上传的 S3 兼容存储以及用于九种语言的 next-intl。 动态 QR 码（与上面的分析相符）是 URL 缩短器：中间件捕获 /u/:shortCode，后端记录扫描并将您 302 发送到目标。

我计划写更多关于各个部分的内容（构建 URL 缩短器、共享架构设置、自托管整个内容）。 在评论中告诉我你真正读过哪一篇。

一年后

一年后，QRcodly 变得稳定，第一批付费客户也出现了——而且我了解到，构建产品可能已经完成了一半的工作。 另一半是确保有人找到它。 这篇文章部分是我在后半部分的工作。

如果这引起了共鸣：我构建了 QRcodly，一个开源 QR 码平台，提供免费的静态和动态 QR 码。 尝试一下，或者来贡献一下：

  • 🔗 qrcodly.de
  • ⭐ github.com/FloB95/qrcodly

二维码

QRcodly 是一个免费、开源的二维码生成器和管理平台。 创建、自定义和跟踪 URL、联系方式、Wi-Fi 凭据等的 QR 码。

特征

  • 多种 QR 码类型 — URL、vCard、Wi-Fi、电子邮件、日历事件、位置、纯文本
  • 完全定制——颜色、尺寸、背景和定制图标上传
  • 导出格式 — PNG、JPEG、SVG
  • URL 缩短和分析 — 缩短链接并跟踪扫描
  • 模板——保存和重复使用二维码配置
  • 自定义域 - 使用您自己的域作为短 URL（Cloudflare 集成）
  • 国际化 — 8 种语言（EN、DE、ES、FR、IT、NL、PL、RU）
  • 身份验证 — 由 Clerk 提供支持
  • 浏览器扩展 — 从任何页面生成二维码

单一回购结构

qrcodly/
├── apps/
│   ├── backend/            # Fastify REST API
│   ├── frontend/           # Next.js web application
│   └── browser-extension/  # Vite-based browser extension
├── packages/
│   ├── shared/             # Zod schemas, DTOs, and shared

…

我很好奇：工具给你带来的最糟糕的“免费直到你依赖它”的伎俩是什么？
